stories.title: Alleged Scattered Spider hacker arrested in Finland
stories.summary: The 19-year-old dual American-Estonian national has been extradited to the US, where he is facing federal charges.

stories.title: Hungarian spy ring targeted EU officials, Commission probe concludes
stories.summary: The Hungarian intelligence operatives sent to Brussels were discreet at first, but starting in 2015, they became more and more overt in their attempts to obtain information.

stories.title: Nincs széna
stories.summary: Az elmúlt évek aszályai már az állattenyésztésben is éreztetik a hatásukat. A takarmány ára duplájára emelkedett az elmúlt időszakban, ami sok gazdának már megfizethetetlen, így az állomány ritkításán gondolkodnak. A szakemberek szerint a probléma megoldásához stratégiai szinten kell újratervezni a hazai takarmánytermesztést.

stories.title: Új vezérigazgató az MTVA-nál: Szentkirályi pártpolitikai átalakításról, Tarr a propaganda megszüntetéséről ír
stories.summary: Sorra érkeznek a reakciók az átmeneti vezérigazgató, Horváth P. András kinevezésére.

stories.title: 200 milliárd forintnyi támogatás érkezik Magyarországra energiakorszerűsítési projektekre
stories.summary: A finanszírozás forrását az uniós kibocsátáskereskedelmi rendszerből származó bevételek jelentik.

stories.title: Felbőszíteni és levideózni: így támadja a Fidesz Magyar Pétert
stories.summary: A házelnök egyfelől bekeményített, és többektől, így a pártja miniszterelnökétől is elvette a szót. Közben viszont elengedte az elődje által kiszabott gigantikus pénzbírságokat. Videós összefoglaló.

stories.title: Előállt egy magyarázattal a kínai vezetés, közel egy héttel azután, hogy repülőgép csapódott a pekingi felhőkarcolóba
stories.summary: A hosszú hallgatás beindította a találgatásokat, de Kína szerint személyes okok álltak a háttérben.

stories.title: A parlamenti házbizottság nem akar beleszólni Magyar és Forsthoffer vitájába
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök zokon vette, hogy a házelnök megvonta tőle a szót. A bizottság csütörtöki ülésén nem is tárgyalt az esetről, és lezártnak tekinti a kérdést.

stories.title: Annak ellenére is jó helyzetben maradhat a Richter, hogy most leépít
stories.summary: Hosszabb távon az EU-ban pont olyan cégek kerülhetnek előnybe, mint a magyar gyártó. Legalábbis akkor, ha a döntéshozók komolyan gondolják az európai gyógyszeripar alapjainak megerősítését.

stories.title: Magyar Péter szerint is le kellene mondania a debreceni polgármesternek
stories.summary: Búcsúzunk a vármegyéktől és a főispánoktól. Magyar Péter reméli, hogy nem lopták el a megyés táblákat. Élőben közvetítettük a kormányszóvivői tájékoztatót.

stories.title: Horvát kapitány: Nem köpködést érdemel a válogatott
stories.summary: Két 40 feletti zseni még sohasem játszott egymás ellen a vb-n, a Modrić–Ronaldo-csata ezért is érdekes lesz a horvát–portugálon.

stories.title: Tízezrek álltak sorban, hogy lássanak egy 11. századi kárpitot
stories.summary: A 1066-os hastingsi csata eseményeit képregényszerűen elbeszélő, csaknem ezer éve készült bayeux-i kárpitot az Egyesült Királyságban most először állítják ki.

stories.title: Kapitány István azonnali vizsgálatot rendelt el, miután két postás meghalt a múlt heti extrém hőség idején
stories.summary: A halálesetek hírét a Magyar Posta is megerősítette. Magyar Péter szerint az előzetes vizsgálat szerint a haláleseteknek nem volt közvetlen köze a hőséghez.

stories.title: Megfeleznék az alkotmánybírók ciklusának a hosszát
stories.summary: Most 12 évre választanak alkotmánybírókat, Magyar Péter szerint ezt hat évre módosítanák – ahogy minden más, Országgyűlés által választott állami tisztviselő megbízatását is. De ha jól végzik a munkájukat, még egy ciklusra újraválaszthatók lesznek.

stories.title: Kártérítésre kötelezték Strasbourgban a magyar államot, mert az ügyészség nem vizsgálta ki elég alaposan egy rendőri bántalmazás gyanúját
stories.summary: Schönberger Ádámnak egy rendőri intézkedés során eltörött egy kézcsontja. Bár alapos gyanú volt rá, hogy bántalmazhatták, az ügyészség megszüntette a nyomozást. Az Emberi Jogok Európai Bírósága szerint nem jártak el kellő alapossággal.

stories.title: Az autópálya-koncessziós vállalat vezetője szerint nem vásároltak követ Orbán Győző cégétől
stories.summary: A Magyar Koncessziós Infrastruktúra Fejlesztő Zrt. vezérigazgatója szerint nem igaz, hogy az előző kormánytól 1000 milliárd forintot kaptak volna.

stories.title: Sulyok és Magyar is tárgyalt a Velencei Bizottsággal, a miniszterelnök szerint nem kaptak kritikát a Sulyokot eltávolító alkotmánymódosításra
stories.summary: A köztársasági elnök eközben arról írt, „méltányolja a Velencei Bizottság elkötelezettségét a vitás helyzet rendezésének támogatásában.”

stories.title: Motortolvajokat kötöz lámpaoszlopokhoz a mexikói duct tape-es Batman, macskabajszot is rajzol az arcukra
stories.summary: A fejük fölé rikító rózsaszín táblára felírja, hogy mit követtek el, az ellopott motorokat pedig közvetlenül a kikötözött tolvajok mellett hagyja a helyiek hőse Batman

stories.title: A Colt CZ-balhé: rettenetesen előnytelen szerződést kötött a magyar állam, ezt próbálja menteni a 4iG
stories.summary: Különös bejelentést tett a 4iG a tőzsdén egy fegyveripari üzletről. Ennek mentünk utána, és kiderült: Magyarország nagyon önsorsrontó volt.

stories.title: Elsőre igen vonzó ez az Airbnb-kalyiba az óceán közepén, de azért felmerül néhány probléma
stories.summary: Például egyrészt sehol senki sincs a közelben, másrészt viszont sehol senki sincs a közelben.

stories.title: Ahol kultúrának számoljuk el a disznóvágást, ott baj van
stories.summary: Nagy Ervin kultúráért felelős államtitkár is részt vett a Telex After Fishing on Orfűn tartott beszélgetésén, ahol a Tisza Párt kultúrprogramjáról, és arról is beszélt, hogy az országban az emberek lelkében léteztek a feketelisták, a tehetség mégis utat tört magának.

stories.title: Venice Commission delegation voiced no criticism of constitutional amendment to remove President, PM Magyar says
stories.summary: Hungarian President Tamás Sulyok had previously requested the body's opinion on the draft of the constitutional amendment which would remove him from office before his term is over.

stories.title: Áder János felszólította Magyar Pétert: bizonyítékot vagy bocsánatkérést vár
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök a sajtótájékoztatóján közpénz „kilapátolásával” vádolta a volt köztársasági elnököt.

stories.title: A fideszes polgármester, aki elmondta Orbánnak a kritikáit – interjú Cser-Palkovics Andrással
stories.summary: Korrupció, arrogáns stílus, rossz mérések – szerinte ezekről is beszélni kellett volna a Fideszben a választási vereség után. 2024-ben már egyértelmű volt számára, hogy változásra van szükség, most pedig beszélni kellene Mészáros Lőrincről és Orbán családjának gazdagodásáról is a Fidesz megújulásához. 36 éve a Fidesz tagja, 16 éve Székesfehérvár polgármestere – interjú Cser-Palkovics Andrással.

stories.title: Miniszterelnökség: Az államtitkárok végkielégítésének kifizetése folyamatban van
stories.summary: „Még véletlenül se jusson eszükbe felvenni a búcsúpénzt, miután tönkretették az országunkat. Akármit is nyilatkoznak, nem fogják megkapni” – mondta korábban Magyar Péter.

stories.title: Gryllus Dorka: Nehéz, amikor valakit szexszimbólumnak kiáltanak ki, de mindenkit azzal biztatok: egyszer elmúlik
stories.summary: Egyszer csak eljön az a pont, hogy a férfiak meghallják azt is, amit mondasz, de odáig nagyon hosszú az út, mondta a színész, rendező az Alapozó című műsor vendégeként. Beszélt a luxushoz fűződő ambivalens viszonyáról és arról is, hogyan éli meg, hogy az 50 feletti nőket mellőzi a filmipar.

stories.title: Why Meloni has hit back hard against Trump and his ‘made up’ photo claim | Riccardo Alcaro
stories.summary: With her popularity flagging and a general election looming, the Italian PM sees a strategic advantage in the rupture If Giorgia Meloni thought that she could put her April spat with Donald Trump over the pope’s criticism of the US war on Iran behind her, she had not banked on the US president’s capacity to bear a grudge. Trump reignited tensions by telling an Italian TV journalist that the Italian PM had “begged” him for a picture at the recent G7 meeting in France. The Spanish newspaper El Paí

stories.title: Hungary: Magyar's 'Operation Purgatory' tackles Orban system
stories.summary: The new Hungarian government is moving swiftly to dismantle the system of former Prime Minister Viktor Orban. The focus: fighting corruption, reforming the media and protecting democracy.

stories.title: Poland orders Swedish submarines to bulk up navy
stories.summary: Poland has placed an order for three Swedish submarines in an effort to step up defense capabilities. The NATO member is modernizing its navy in the face of Russian aggression at its borders.

stories.title: German prosecutors accuse Kyiv of ordering 2022 Nord Stream sabotage
stories.summary: Indictment against alleged leader of gas pipeline attack claims former Ukrainian army officer was directed by state German prosecutors have accused Ukrainian “state authorities” of ordering the 2022 explosives attack on the Nord Stream gas pipelines linking Russia with Europe, a charge likely to ignite tensions between Kyiv and Berlin, its biggest military backer. The sabotage in the Baltic Sea by a team of assailants almost entirely destroyed the seafloor infrastructure of the key source of Rus

stories.title: At least 21 dead as Russia launches massive drone and missile attack on Kyiv
stories.summary: More than 70 missiles fired at Ukraine capital as Russia faces fuel shortages after strikes against its oil refineries At least 21 people were killed and dozens injured overnight in Kyiv, local authorities said, in what the city’s mayor, Vitali Klitschko, called the worst Russian attack on the capital during more than four years of air assault on Ukraine. Russia used nearly 500 drones and more than 70 missiles in the hours-long attack on Kyiv and other parts of the country in the early hours of

stories.title: Death toll rises to 21 with more than 80 injured in huge Russian attack on Kyiv –as it happened
stories.summary: Damage recorded in 30 locations across in the city from overnight attacks, with ‘most of them ordinary residential buildings’ in Dublin Volodymyr Zelenskyy has said he hopes not to wait too long for the results of an Irish government investigation into alumina exports to Russia thought to be feeding the Kremlin’s war machine. “Unfortunately there are companies in Europe that are owned or effectively controlled by Russia and its sanctioned oligarchs. They keep supplying the aggressor with essenti

stories.title: Outrage over ‘cruel’ Florida move to ban undocumented students from college
stories.summary: Critics condemn decision by education board aligned with hard-right Ron DeSantis to block access to college system Immigration advocates in Florida have decried a “cruel and harmful” new rule by education officials aligned with hard-right Republican governor Ron DeSantis to ban undocumented students from state colleges and universities. The Florida board of education voted on Tuesday to bar access to its 28 state-funded institutions to anybody not a US citizen or “lawfully present” in the countr

stories.title: Infrastructure cuts to pay for defence will cost UK 10,000 jobs, analysis shows
stories.summary: Exclusive: Findings cast doubt on Starmer claims that reallocation of funds to MoD will boost British jobs Keir Starmer’s decision to cut billions of pounds of infrastructure spending to pay for more defence equipment will end up costing the UK 10,000 jobs, according to an analysis of the government’s own figures. The prime minister announced this week he was putting an extra £15bn into defence investment to revamp the country’s armed forces and boost British manufacturing. Continue reading...

stories.title: Former Olympian canoeist reportedly indicted after reaching into reflecting pool – US politics live
stories.summary: David Hearn was arrested on misdemeanor charge as Trump’s $14m renovation of Washington DC landmark appeared to be botched Full report: Trump hijacked US’s 250 anniversary to serve ‘political ideology and pet projects’ Sign up for the Breaking News US email OpenAI is reportedly in early stage talks to give a 5% stake in the ChatGPT developer to the US government as artificial intelligence companies attempt to smooth relations with Donald Trump’s administration. The OpenAI chief executive, Sam Al

stories.title: Albanian police fire tear gas at 'Flamingo Revolution' rally
stories.summary: Albanians have continued to protest against a planned luxury resort backed by Donald Trump's son-in-law, Jared Kushner. They say the resort threatens the coast's fragile ecosystems, including a flamingo habitat.

stories.title: Côte d’Ivoire floods kill 59 as west Africa endures torrential rains
stories.summary: Authorities say rainy season getting deadlier, with Ghana reporting 13 dead and floods hitting Benin, Togo and Nigeria Floods in Côte d’Ivoire have killed 59 people since May, the communication minister told a cabinet meeting in Abidjan. There are fears the toll could further rise as rescue teams continue to search for victims during the rainy season, which runs from May until July, the minister, Amadou Coulibaly, added. Continue reading...

stories.title: Papua separatists kill American pilot in ‘message’ to US and Indonesia
stories.summary: Rebels shoot pilot and set his civilian ⁠plane on fire amid long-running low-level battle for independence in region Separatist rebels in Indonesia’s restive easternmost region of Papua have shot dead an American pilot and set a civilian ⁠plane on fire, in what a spokesperson for a local militant group described as a “message” to the US and Indonesian governments. Sebby Sambom, ⁠a spokesperson for the West Papua National Liberation Army (TPNPB), named the pilot as Nicholas F Gosselin and said se

stories.title: AFP assessing allegation of Gaza war crimes committed by Australian citizen serving in IDF
stories.summary: Claims against Israel Defense Forces battalion include deliberate targeting and destruction of residential buildings in 2023 and 2024 without military justification Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Australian federal police investigators are assessing a brief detailing alleged war crimes committed by an Australian citizen serving in the Israel Defense Forces in Gaza. A 61-page brief has been handed to the AFP by the Australian Centre for International Justice detailin

stories.title: Man accused of ordering Daphne Caruana Galizia murder paid hitmen’s legal fees, court hears
stories.summary: Yorgen Fenech said to have spent €400,000 on fees for men convicted of car bombing that killed investigative journalist A businessman accused of commissioning the murder of the Maltese journalist Daphne Caruana Galizia spent more than €400,000 (£343,000) on legal fees for the hitmen convicted of her killing, prosecutors claim. Yorgen Fenech, the 44-year-old heir to one of Malta’s largest fortunes, arrived in court for the second day of his trial on Thursday in an unmarked armoured police vehicle

stories.title: Germany news: Coalition agrees package to boost growth
stories.summary: Chancellor Friedrich Merz says his coalition has cleared a reform package as pressure grows to deliver growth. Meanwhile, a top court rejects complaints against the criminalization of childlike sex dolls. DW has more.

stories.title: Nord Stream blast ordered by Ukraine, say German prosecutors
stories.summary: German prosecutors believe the Ukrainian national suspected of sabotaging Baltic Sea gas pipelines in 2022 acted "on the orders of state authorities in Ukraine."

stories.title: Turkish comedian Deniz Goktas detained at Istanbul airport
stories.summary: Turkish stand-up comedian Deniz Goktas was detained on his return to Turkey, days after a video he posted online gained traction. The clip included references to President Recep Tayyip Erdogan and religion.

stories.title: KNDS: German-French tank manufacturer postpones IPO
stories.summary: The German government had been set to purchase a 40% stake in KNDS, but the prerequisite stock market flotation has been postponed. Meanwhile, a German drone start-up has raised $1 billion in fresh funding.

stories.title: Smoke rises over Kyiv after Russian overnight strikes
stories.summary: Smoke rises over Kyiv after a large-scale Russian missile and drone attack overnight that killed at least eight people and injured dozens.

stories.title: South Africa and Ghana in diplomatic row over alleged killing of migrant
stories.summary: South Africa denies claims that a Ghanaian citizen was killed in anti-migrant protests in Cape Town.

stories.title: The US deported them to Venezuela - hours later earthquakes struck
stories.summary: They landed on Flight 164 just before the twin quakes. Family members are desperately searching for them.

stories.title: Von der Leyen unveils major EU support initiatives in strategic trip to Armenia
stories.summary: European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen announced a significant economic and political support package to Armenia during her strategic visit to engage with the South Caucasus peace process and the new opportunities in the region formerly in Russia’s orbit.

stories.title: Ryanair warns of 'queue chaos' from new EU border system
stories.summary: The airline calls on European governments to postpone the EES system until after the summer holiday period.

stories.title: François Durvye
stories.summary: Son passage à plein temps au RN, dont il était conseiller officieux depuis 2021, a été quelque peu chahuté par les mauvais échos sur sa gestion d’Otium, le fonds d’investissement de Pierre-Edouard Stérin. Le milliardaire a néanmoins défendu son ancien bras droit, tout en le dépeignant en étatiste peu libéral. Qu’importe, le polytechnicien est vite passé à autre chose, s’attelant à rapprocher le parti à la flamme du monde des affaires. D’abord avec un fameux dîner entre Marine Le Pen et des patro

stories.title: Arthur Mensch
stories.summary: Alors que l’entreprise d’intelligence artificielle qu’il a cofondée a soufflé sa troisième bougie, Arthur Mensch poursuit sa mutation de chercheur en intelligence artificielle à oracle des dépendances européennes en matière technologique. Devant l’Assemblée, début mai, il a enchaîné une série de constats et de recommandations en faveur d’une meilleure autonomie européenne dans ce domaine. La séquence est devenue virale, car elle questionne l’intérêt réel des politiques face aux enjeu

stories.title: Benjamin Morel
stories.summary: Dans la vie de ce constitutionnaliste, il y a eu un avant et un après 9 juin 2024. La dissolution de l’Assemblée nationale l’a transformé, à 38 ans, en distributeur d’analyses et de remèdes juridicopolitiques. Un brin cocasse, quand on sait que sa thèse, préfacée par Gérard Larcher, porte sur la légitimité du Sénat. L’encre de son dernier livre Crise politique, Crise de régime (Odile Jacob) à peine sèche, il finalise déjà son prochain manuscrit sur la crise… de l’Etat. Entre deux cours de droit

stories.title: Flavie Flament
stories.summary: Depuis plus de trente ans, les Français la connaissent comme présentatrice d’une vingtaine d’émissions, parmi lesquelles Stars à domicile (TF1). Au-delà de la télé, c’est son combat contre les violences sexuelles qui a donné de l’écho à sa parole. Cette année, son témoignage à visage découvert et sa plainte pour viol contre Patrick Bruel, déposée le 13 mai, ont contribué à la mise en examen du chanteur pour viol, tentatives de viol, d’agression sexuelle et de harcèlement sexuel, alors que plusie

stories.title: Spain’s opposition accuses Sánchez of electoral engineering after migrant legalization drive
stories.summary: Leader of Spain’s center-right People’s Party Alberto Núñez Feijóo alleged Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez aimed to use migrants to expand his voter base following the government’s recent mass migrant legalization effort. “If we add up the number of immigrants who have entered Spain between 2019 and 2025, it’s four million, and now we’re adding 1.3 million with this legalization drive, and the 2.5 million that the government calls grandchildren,” the conservative leade

stories.title: Understanding Ebola’s wildlife origins is crucial to preventing next big outbreak
stories.summary: If we don’t know the source, not only do humans remain at risk but wildlife can suffer needlessly via retaliation While virologists and public health departments were palpitating over the news of an Andes virus infectious disease outbreak on a cruise ship (13 cases, three deaths), in the Democratic Republic of the Congo the Bundibugyo virus, the root of the current Ebola outbreak (currently more than 1,250 cases and at least 362 deaths), was smouldering under the radar. Bundibugyo virus is a hor

stories.title: Young Indonesian couple publicly caned after kissing on TikTok
stories.summary: Unmarried man and woman whipped 21 times each because they had violated province’s version of Islamic law A young couple in Indonesia’s conservative Aceh province have been publicly caned after a Sharia court convicted them of violating Islamic law by kissing during a TikTok livestream. The court ordered the couple, a 22-year-old man and a 25-year-old woman, to be whipped with a rattan cane 21 times each for kissing without being married. At least 100 people witnessed the caning, carried out by

stories.title: Social media platforms ‘monetise gore and fringe content’, eSafety regulator tells antisemitism commission
stories.summary: Julie Inman Grant singles out X when giving evidence to royal commission on antisemitism and social cohesion Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Mainstream platforms are fighting to distribute and monetise “gore” and “fringe” content, the eSafety watchdog has told the antisemitism royal commission. Australia’s eSafety commissioner, Julie Inman Grant, on Thursday singled out X, telling the inquiry her office has to fight its billionaire owner, Elon Musk, to try to keep fo

stories.title: At least nine monks killed in Thailand after boy drives truck into procession
stories.summary: Charges yet to be filed over incident in Mukdahan involving 11-year-old, as police seek to establish circumstances An 11-year-old boy has driven his parents’ truck into a Buddhist procession in Thailand, killing at least nine monks. CCTV footage shared by a local rescue group showed the moment the monks, wearing orange robes, were run over as they walked in procession along a road. The timestamp on the footage was shortly before 11am local time on Thursday. Continue reading...

stories.title: Spain and France face more heat after scorching June caused 2,000 deaths
stories.summary: Temperatures could reach 44C in south-east Spain as fights break out in Paris supermarkets over air-conditioning units Spain and France are bracing for another possible heatwave that could bring temperatures of 44C (111F) over the coming days, as figures show that June’s extreme heat was responsible for more than 2,000 excess deaths in the two countries. Spain’s state meteorological agency, Aemet, said a mass of dry and very hot air would bring persistently high temperatures to much of Spain fro

stories.title: Bayeux tapestry tickets generate nine-hour online queues as public scramble for access
stories.summary: Reports say up to 80,000 people waiting by mid-afternoon for chance to see historic artwork at British Museum People keen to see the Bayeux tapestry faced online queues of up to nine hours when tickets went on sale for the first time on Wednesday morning. The British Museum, which is hosting the tapestry from September, saw huge traffic to its ticketing website as a scramble for access began. Continue reading...

stories.title: Vatican excommunicates all members of ultra-conservative rebel group SSPX
stories.summary: Schism caused by Society of Saint Pius X ordaining four bishops without consent presents first crisis for Pope Leo Europe live – latest updates The Vatican has excommunicated a rebel group of ultra-conservative Catholics who defied Pope Leo by ordaining bishops without his consent, creating a schism in the Roman Catholic church. In a statement on Thursday, Cardinal Víctor Manuel Fernández, who heads the Holy See’s Dicastery for the Doctrine of the Faith, said the group from the Society of Saint

stories.title: Australians have more negative view of Israel than of China, Guardian Essential poll finds
stories.summary: Poll also finds perception of Donald Trump has soured since his re-election, with fewer than a third of respondents viewing him positively Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Australians have a more negative view of Israel than of China, the latest Guardian Essential poll has found, with women and middle-aged people exhibiting the least positive perception of Israel. The poll also finds Australians’ perceptions of Donald Trump have tumbled since his re-election, with few

stories.title: Australia’s median wealth falls almost 7% since 2020 despite the rich getting richer, report says
stories.summary: Swiss bank UBS finds ‘growing divide between the wealthiest and the broader population’ as country adds more than 25,000 more millionaires Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Middle Australia’s wealth is going backwards while the richest claim an ever-greater share of the pile, according to new research that shows more than 25,000 people across the country became millionaires last year. The latest global wealth report from the Swiss bank UBS said 2025 marked “an extraord

stories.title: ‘I don’t just watch climate change happening’: the young Swedes being paid to make a difference
stories.summary: Participant-led YPS scheme creates green projects while providing summer jobs in country with high youth unemployment Oona Verveld and Clara Vikberg have just secured their first paid summer jobs. While their peers are mostly limited to entry-level positions in retail or fast-food restaurants, the 18-year-olds are some of the first among their generation to have landed a new type of role: young planetary stewards. “Someone came up with the simple idea that, since young people clearly need jobs,

stories.title: AOC endorses progressive Democrat in closely watched Michigan race for US Senate seat
stories.summary: Abdul El-Sayed, backed by Bernie Sanders, leads polls ahead of Haley Stevens and Mallory McMorrow in primary Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez has thrown her support behind Abdul El-Sayed, the doctor and progressive Democrat seeking the party’s nomination in Michigan’s closely watched US Senate race. In an interview with the New York Times, Ocasio-Cortez – an influential congresswoman on the left of the Democratic party – endorsed El-Sayed, a former public health director. “Despite our ideological differ

stories.title: Novice climber rescued after surviving 1,500ft fall down California mountain
stories.summary: The woman, 31, suffered some injuries but was found alert and in good spirits on Mount Shasta, officials said A novice climber was rescued after surviving a 1,500ft fall down California’s Mount Shasta on Sunday, officials said. The woman, 31, was attempting to ascend the mountain along the Left of Heart variation of the popular Avalanche Gulch route alongside two other novice climbers at an elevation of about 13,000ft when she fell. Continue reading...

stories.title: Canadian boy dies of rabies after waking to find bat on his face
stories.summary: Eleven-year-old developed symptoms 19 days after encounter in Ontario in ‘exceedingly rare’ case Doctors in Canada say a child who awoke to find a bat resting on his nose and mouth while visiting an Ontario cottage later died of rabies, in an “exceedingly rare case” that highlights the need for better public awareness. In a report published this week in the Canadian Medical Association Journal, infectious disease physicians confirmed that the 11-year-old boy died from rabies, a fatality they sai

stories.title: ‘Truly international’ network of drug-facilitated rape uncovered by UK crime agency
stories.summary: NCA says offenders arrange to sexually assault and film victims via online networks with crimes often taking place in trusting relationships Criminal investigators in the UK say they have uncovered a “truly international network” of organised drug-facilitated sexual assault in which victims are sedated before being raped and sexually assaulted. The National Crime Agency [NCA] has said online networks, “many as yet unidentified by law enforcement”, were allowing offenders to arrange to rape and a

stories.title: Venezuelan man saved from collapsed mall eight days after earthquakes
stories.summary: Security guard Hernán Alberto Gil Flores, 43, initially told rescuers not to tell his wife in case he did not survive A 43-year-old security guard who survived last week’s devastating earthquakes in Venezuela thanks to a pocket of air in his workstation cabin has been pulled from the collapsed basement of a shopping centre amid huge cheers from international rescue teams. Hernán Alberto Gil Flores had been trapped for eight days under the rubble of the Galerías Playa Grande in the hard-hit coast

stories.title: ‘Hugely significant’: those affected by forced adoptions welcome apology but demand more remedy
stories.summary: Victims say hearing Keir Starmer’s acknowledgment of their suffering was emotional but want more mental support This summer’s World Cup fever vividly takes Ann Keen back to 1966 and the day England won the tournament – she was 17 years old and it was the day she told her father she was pregnant. “It was the worst thing that could ever have been said to him. I was told I’d put shame on the family and I must be sent away,” the former Labour MP said. “I was in an unmarried mother’s home where I had

stories.title: Lisa Nandy quits X over fears Musk-owned site pushes ‘abuse and misinformation’
stories.summary: Culture secretary says her department will stop using platform, citing concerns over far-right content fuelling violence and division The UK’s culture and media department will stop using X because the site “now favours abuse and misinformation over meaningful debate”, Lisa Nandy has announced. The culture secretary’s department is the UK’s second to quit the Elon Musk-owned platform over increasing concerns about the way it highlights and prioritises often inaccurate far-right and racist conten

stories.title: Sitting for more than 30 minutes at a time linked to higher risk of cancer death
stories.summary: Study suggests even light activity such as ironing could reduce health risks linked to prolonged sedentary behaviour Sitting for longer than half an hour at a time each day raises the risk of dying from cancer, a study suggests. Researchers who tracked more than 90,000 people over a decade found that sitting or lying down while awake for more than 30 minutes in one period each day was associated with an increased risk of cancer death. The risk increases for every additional hour of continuous in

stories.title: Burnham ‘coronation’ will anger some Labour members, party bosses warned
stories.summary: NEC told it must take steps to address fears – including not hiring Everton stadium to announce new leader before nominations even open Labour chiefs have been warned they must placate disgruntled Labour members who are angry at the lack of party democracy because Andy Burnham is not expected to face a challenge to become Labour leader. MPs have told the party there are growing complaints from members about the lack of involvement from members if Burnham does not face a leadership contest from a

stories.title: India news: Narendra Modi hosts Japan's Sanae Takaichi
stories.summary: Sanae Takaichi is on her first visit to India since becoming PM, and has held talks with Narendra Modi to boost bilateral ties.

stories.title: Thailand: Child crashes vehicle into group of monks
stories.summary: An 11-year-old boy crashed a pickup truck belonging to his parents into a group of monks on a pilgrimage, police said. Eight were killed in the incident and 14 more were hospitalized in Mukdahan province.

stories.title: Fact check: Racist German fan petition story false
stories.summary: It has been claimed that following Germany's World Cup exit, some fans launched a petition seeking to ban "African" and "Muslim" players from the national team — and that DW reported the story. This is not true.

stories.title: Vatican excommunicates rebel SSPX bishops, followers
stories.summary: The Vatican's watchdog authority has imposed severe disciplinary measures on an ultratraditionalist Catholic group. The move comes after the group, the Society of Saint Pius X, consecrated bishops without papal consent.

stories.title: Mexico: The 2026 World Cup's best host?
stories.summary: Mexico has fully embraced its role as a World Cup host. Many visitors rave about the atmosphere and wish there were more knockout-stage matches in Mexico as opposed to the United States.

stories.title: Syria: Bomb blast at Damascus cafe kills several
stories.summary: At least six people were killed when an explosive device detonated near a key government building in the Syrian capital.

stories.title: EU targets Chinese imports amid trade talks
stories.summary: Brussels has ended a customs exemption for low-value imports from China while agreeing with Beijing to monitor trade flows and improve access to rare earth materials.

stories.title: Iran turns Khamenei funeral into show of power
stories.summary: Iran's leadership is planning a six-day funeral for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei starting July 4, in what officials say will be the largest gathering in Tehran's history — and a demonstration of strength at home and abroad.

stories.title: Russia launches attacks on Kyiv, killing at least 21 and injuring scores
stories.summary: Ballistic and cruise missiles and drones were used in what Kyiv's mayor, Vitali Klitschko, said was the largest attack on the city since the war began.

stories.title: Police identify 156 in drug-facilitated sexual assault cases
stories.summary: An international operation led by German and British police has uncovered 156 suspects and victims in cases of drug-facilitated sexual assault. Operation Medusa has led to 57 arrests and 158 safeguarded victims so far.

stories.title: First Islamic‑theological faculty at a public university
stories.summary: The University of Münster is the first public university in Europe to establish a faculty of Islamic theology, which is attracting international attention.

stories.title: German coalition agrees on sweeping reform package
stories.summary: After its first year in office, Germany's coalition government is seeking to revive a sluggish economy and push through key changes with its broad package of reforms. The plans met with a mixed reaction.

stories.title: Venezuelan man rescued alive 8 days after powerful quakes
stories.summary: Rescuers worked around the clock for three days to free security guard Hernan Gil from the rubble of the building where he worked.

stories.title: After heat wave: Wildfires ravage southern France
stories.summary: Firefighters battle several wildfires in southern France that were fueled by strong winds. Nearly 3,000 tourists and locals were evacuated.

stories.title: Explosion in Monaco: Who is Vadym Yermolaiev?
stories.summary: Ukrainian-born businessman Vadym Yermolaiev was reportedly injured in a suspected assassination attempt in Monaco. Kyiv had previously imposed sanctions on him. What is known about the case?

stories.title: US fans celebrate World Cup last-16 spot
stories.summary: US supporters celebrate in Carson, California, after the United States beat Bosnia-Herzegovina 2-0 to reach the World Cup round of 16.

stories.title: China says pilot crashed small plane into skyscraper for 'personal reasons'
stories.summary: The 66-year-old, who died in the crash, had anxiety and referenced "ending his life" in his diary.

stories.title: Fast-moving wildfire spreads across southern France
stories.summary: Firefighters battle a fast-moving wildfire in Aude, southern France, after flames spread from Hérault and burned more than 800 hectares overnight.

stories.title: Any deal with Tehran is 'a pause before the next war,' Reza Pahlavi tells Euronews
stories.summary: In an exclusive interview with Euronews, Reza Pahlavi said the framework agreement would allow the Islamic Republic to regroup and rearm, with the only winner from the deal being the regime in Tehran.

stories.title: Nine Thai monks killed after 11-year-old driver collides with procession
stories.summary: The group of 35 monks and five lay followers were walking by the roadside when the truck ploughed into them.

stories.title: 11-year-old boy dies from rabies in Canada after waking up to a bat on his face
stories.summary: Rabies is a rare but deadly infection usually spread by a bite or scratch from an infected animal. It is almost always fatal after the onset of symptoms, although vaccination and early treatment can help to prevent it.

stories.title: Watch: BBC at site of deadly Russian attack on Kyiv flats
stories.summary: Sarah Rainsford reports from the completely destroyed nine-storey block of flats in Ukraine's capital.

stories.title: Damascus explosion caused by bomb, state TV says, as death toll climbs to six
stories.summary: The explosion comes a day after the first parliament since the fall of long-time leader Bashar al-Assad began to take shape.

stories.title: Canada celebrates its birthday as Mark Carney battles to keep it intact
stories.summary: As Canadians celebrate their nation's birthday, independence movements in two provinces are eyeing the exit from the country.

stories.title: Cheers and applause as security guard rescued from rubble
stories.summary: Emergency workers managed to free Hernán Gil more than 100 hours after they had first located him under 140 tonnes of rubble.

stories.title: Residents of Ethiopian town forced to kill hundreds of their own dogs after rabies deaths
stories.summary: Some residents reluctantly hung their dogs to avoid being arrested or fined, locals tell the BBC.

stories.title: Iran renews Hormuz threats as Doha talks end with cautious progress
stories.summary: Tehran threatened a "forceful response" against vessels straying from its approved Hormuz routes, even as mediators said indirect US-Iran talks in Doha concluded with "positive progress". The next round of talks will wait until after Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's funeral, which begins on Saturday.

stories.title: Venezuela quake survivor pulled out alive after eight days
stories.summary: Hernán Gil was trapped under a collapsed multi-storey car park, and encouraged them as they inched closer to him.

stories.title: French aerobatic team stages flypast over New York for US anniversary
stories.summary: Patrouille de France flies over New York with Alpha Jets and coloured smoke during celebrations marking the 250th anniversary of the US Declaration of Independence.

stories.title: UN to close its human rights office in Burkina Faso following junta's suspension
stories.summary: Junta chief captain Ibrahim Traoré has pursued anti-Western policies since seizing power in a coup in September 2022, while critical voices are met with repression.

stories.title: Venezuelan security guard pulled alive from basement eight days after twin quakes
stories.summary: The earthquakes destroyed tens of thousands of buildings across northern Venezuela, killing more than 2,200 people, and leaving La Guaira state as the hardest-hit region in the country.

stories.title: Vatican excommunicates followers of global Catholic sect
stories.summary: Around 600,000 followers of the Society of Saint Pius X, a Catholic sect, are affected.

stories.title: Bomb blast at Damascus cafe kills six, Syrian state media say
stories.summary: There was no immediate claim for the bombing at a cafe close to the Palace of Justice, a major government building.

stories.title: 'Most massive' Russian attack on Kyiv kills at least 25
stories.summary: The latest barrage deployed the largest number of weapons on the capital and hit locations over a wide area.

stories.title: Killers of British couple in South Africa sentenced to life
stories.summary: The pair had been collecting rare plants and seeds before their bodies were found days later in a river.

stories.title: Nicolás Maduro sued in US court over alleged extrajudicial killings in Venezuela
stories.summary: Maduro is currently in a New York jail awaiting trial on criminal charges of drug trafficking after the US military removed him from office in an extraordinary raid in Venezuela in January.

stories.title: EU looks to South Caucasus for solutions as von der Leyen holds talks with President Aliyev
stories.summary: Von der Leyen begins her South Caucasus tour in Baku as the EU steps up engagement on regional stability, energy and connectivity.

stories.title: Turkish police beat us with iron rods before we lost limbs to frostbite, Afghans say
stories.summary: The young migrants heading to Europe say they were stripped and had their hands tied in freezing temperatures.

stories.title: Alleged member of hacking group 'Scattered Spider' arrested in Finland
stories.summary: Scattered Spider, also known as “Octo Tempest,” “UNC3944,” or “0ktapus,” is a group of criminal cyber actors that attempts to extort ransoms from companies.

stories.title: Nicolas Dufourcq
stories.summary: Pour lui, la France est au bord du précipice et la prochaine présidentielle “est l’élection la plus importante depuis 30 ans”. Ainsi Nicolas Dufourcq, dont le job est d’investir dans l’économie tricolore (pour des montants chaque année plus considérables ), s’est-il fait oracle auprès des décideurs publics, cette année : si personne ne fait reculer la dette, prophétise-t-il dans son dernier livre , nous vivrons “un drame qui peut nous faire sortir de l’histoire”. Le sexagénaire, qui connaît tout

stories.title: Julia Cagé
stories.summary: Ça ne lui fait pas peur, assure-t-elle, mais en 2027, entre une union de la gauche inexistante et des rédactions qui peinent à garantir l’indépendance éditoriale, Julia Cagé aura fort à faire. La professeure d’économie à Sciences Po est engagée, de longue date, en faveur d’un rassemblement des électeurs de gauche. En 2024, quelques heures après l’annonce de la dissolution par Emmanuel Macron, elle fédérait des centaines de signatures autour d’un appel à l’union et au sursaut. Une tribune d

stories.title: Rodolphe Saadé
stories.summary: Qu’importe les eaux troubles de la mondialisation, le troisième armateur mondial croit en l’avenir : il vient d’inaugurer le Notre-Dame , soit le plus gros porte-conteneurs de sa flotte de 700 navires. Plus riche que jamais, Rodolphe Saadé collectionne les médias (déjà BFMTV, RMC, La Tribune, La Provence et Brut à son actif) et les breloques : il est devenu cette année officier de la Légion d’honneur, des mains d’Emmanuel Macron, of course . Le chef de l’Etat a loué un “négociateur hors pair”, “

stories.title: Vincent Bolloré
stories.summary: C’est maintenant ou jamais. Après l’échec de la fusée Zemmour en 2022, Vincent Bolloré se rêve toujours en faiseur de président. L’ogre des médias (Cnews, Europe 1, Le JDD), de l’édition (Hachette, Lagardère, Fayard) et de la com (Havas) a désormais ajouté son propre think tank à son empire : l’Institut de l’espérance. Un cercle de réflexion catholique, dont les idées pour influencer l’élection reine vont du recul de l’immigration à la fin du délit d’entrave à l’avortement, en passant par la pré

stories.title: Andy Burnham is about to have a crash course in hardcore geopolitics | Rafael Behr
stories.summary: The likely next prime minister would rather be in Warrington than Washington, but foreign policy will dominate his agenda more than he thinks If Andy Burnham is lucky, he will get some time to adjust to the pace of life as prime minister before dealing with his first international crisis. It won’t be long. Donald Trump is an engine of constant turmoil. Aftershocks from his recent military adventure in the Middle East will be felt for years. None of the declared war aims were achieved. The Irania

stories.title: Sudan’s RSF committed crimes against humanity in El Fasher, Amnesty says
stories.summary: Report accuses paramilitary force of crimes including ethnic cleansing in systemic campaign against civilians The Sudanese paramilitary Rapid Support Forces committed crimes against humanity and ethnic cleansing during its campaign to capture El Fasher, Amnesty International has alleged. Many of the crimes, including murder, torture, rape, enslavement and sexual slavery, were carried out as part of a widespread and systematic attack against civilians and amounted to crimes against humanity, the

stories.title: Ahead of NATO summit, Turkey arrests more than 200 activists
stories.summary: Protesters are clearly unwelcome ahead of the upcoming NATO summit in Ankara. Amid a strict public gatherings ban, 225 human rights and environmental activists as well as journalists have already been arrested.

stories.title: Russia's fuel crisis: Is Putin under pressure?
stories.summary: Ukraine's extended campaign against Russian energy infrastructure is disrupting fuel supplies and military logistics. With shortages mounting in occupied Crimea, is the pressure pushing Putin toward negotiations?

stories.title: Trump refuses to renew US-Canada-Mexico trade pact he once championed
stories.summary: Trump and US officials opted to keep USMCA alive on short leash of annual reviews rather than longer term renewal Donald Trump has refused to renew the North American trade pact he once championed as his signature deal, opting instead to keep it alive on a short leash of annual reviews rather than committing to another 16 years. Wednesday was the deadline built into the United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement (USMCA) for the three countries to jointly decide its fate, which is set to expire in 203

stories.title: China’s ethnic unity law denounced as ‘forced assimilation’ by rights groups
stories.summary: Law comes into effect that critics fear will further erode rights of Uyghurs and Tibetans, as well as allow Beijing to pursue dissidents abroad A new ethnic unity law has come into effect in China despite warnings from Taiwan, the United Nations and rights groups that it could threaten freedoms, especially for minorities. The Law on Promoting Ethnic Unity and Progress aims to forge a “shared” national identity among ethnic groups, for example by strengthening the status of Mandarin as the offici

stories.title: US declines to renew USMCA trade pact with Mexico, Canada
stories.summary: Lengthy negotiations are expected as the Trump administration seeks changes to a trade deal with Mexico and Canada that has streamlined supply chains across North America.

stories.title: Hospital fire in northern Germany kills two
stories.summary: A fire at a hospital in the northern German town of Ludwigslust has killed two people. Many patients and staff were evacuated from the facility.

stories.title: US rail boom still lags behind cars and planes
stories.summary: As gas prices fluctuate and flights get more expensive, travelers in the US are rediscovering trains — only to find a system built to move boxes, not people.

stories.title: California to institute Bruce Lee Day, first for a Chinese-American in state history
stories.summary: In the 1960s, Lee found work in Hollywood, notably as Kato in the TV series "The Green Hornet," but studios wanted him to play racist stereotypes and paid him less than his white counterparts.

stories.title: Banned by Beijing, this comedian is taking his act to Chinese speakers abroad
stories.summary: Chizi, one of China’s biggest stand-up comedians, has found a new stage after falling afoul of China's censors.

stories.title: From Truman's pension to Trump's billions - a White House windfall unmatched by any president
stories.summary: Historians say Trump's $2.2bn income last year is unprecedented and blurs the line on conflicts of interest.

stories.title: From friendship bracelets to wedding rings: A Taylor and Travis timeline
stories.summary: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are expected to celebrate their wedding with a party at New York's Madison Square Garden this weekend.

stories.title: Aunt of Venezuelan boy pulled from rubble tells BBC she will give him 'mother's warmth'
stories.summary: Kleiber Moran, 2, was rescued on Wednesday, six days after last week's deadly earthquakes.

stories.title: Did this AI anti-drug video make drugs look appealing?
stories.summary: Hong Kong’s Correctional Services Department removed an AI-generated K-pop video, after facing backlash that it made substances look too appealing.

stories.title: US-Iran parallel talks in Qatar end with pledge to keep talking
stories.summary: Holding separate meetings in Qatar, the US and Iran made progress and agreed that the next meeting would be after Khamenei's funeral, host Qatar confirmed.

stories.title: Canadian boy, 11, dies of rabies after waking to bat on his face
stories.summary: Rabies infections are rare in Canada. There have been 28 human deaths due to rabies in the country since 1924.

stories.title: Venezuela's death toll at least 2,295 as medical crisis widens
stories.summary: Doctors fear a severe medical crisis in Venezuela following the 24 June earthquakes that killed 2,295 and injured more than 11,000. Thousands of displaced survivors are living in crowded shelters without clean water.

stories.title: 'Beautiful and terrible' - the world's thoughts on the US at 250
stories.summary: From Brazil to India to Egypt, the BBC asked people across the world what they think of America today.

stories.title: Gaza patients face agonising delays for medical evacuation
stories.summary: An estimated 300 Palestinians referred for treatment abroad have died since the ceasefire began, according to Gaza's Hamas-run health ministry.

stories.title: People smuggler convicted in France found by BBC living in UK and seeking asylum
stories.summary: Once described as "the godfather" of French migrant camps, he has been working in a Leicestershire village.

stories.title: Russia's petrol crisis worsens as citizens feel the bite of Moscow's war in Ukraine
stories.summary: A third of Russia's refining capacity has been knocked out by Ukrainian drone attacks, causing petrol shortages across more than half of Russia's regions.

stories.title: Couple climbs Empire State Building, gets engaged, then arrested
stories.summary: Two climbers scaled the Empire State Building's antenna, displayed a peace banner and appeared to get engaged before being arrested by New York police.

stories.title: Kiközösítette a Vatikán a pápai engedély nélkül Svájcban felszentelt ultrakonzervatív püspököket
stories.summary: XIV. Leó pápa korábban már nyílt levélben figyelmeztette őket a következményekre.

stories.title: Hollywoodi szuperhősfilmet idéz, ahogy a vb aranycipőjéért zajló harc alakul
stories.summary: Eddig tökéletes a FIFA számára a világbajnokság: Messi, Mbappé, Kane és Haaland versenyez a gólkirályi címért, a lelátók pedig tömve.

stories.title: Szabadstrandi minifesztivál, írók-költők a Balatonon és csillagfényes kertmozi
stories.summary: E heti kulturális ajánlónkban többségben vannak a vízközeli programok. Persze józsefvárosi nőtörténeteket is hallgathatunk, és egy izgalmas könyvbemutatóra is beülhetünk.

stories.title: A fogások egy része olyan csípős, hogy a szegediek nem tudják megenni
stories.summary: Több a kínai vendég, új termékek kerülnek a polcokra, az étlapokon pedig megjelentek az autentikusabb fogások. A régi szegedi ázsiai vállalkozások már érzik a BYD hatását.

stories.title: A tiszás képviselők lemondásra szólították fel Debrecen polgármesterét, visszautasította a vádakat
stories.summary: Debrecen polgármestere felszólította a Semcorpot, hogy távozzon a városból, ezután a tiszás képviselők felszólították őt, hogy mondjon le.

stories.title: Biró Ferenc bizarrnak tartja, hogy Balásy cége is elindult az Integritás Hatóság antikorrupciós kampányára kiírt közbeszerzésen
stories.summary: Aztán a NER kedvenc kommunikációs cége még be is perelte Biróékat. Az Integritás Hatóság elnöke volt a vendége Krug Emília Szinten túl című műsorának.

stories.summary: A hidegfront véget vetett a 11 napos, melegrekordokat döntő hőhullámnak, de bármikor jöhet a következő. Mit kéne sürgősen tennie az Élő Környezetért Felelős Minisztériumnak és a kormánynak, hogy az ilyeneket a lehető legkevésbé szenvedje meg az ország?

stories.title: Változtattak az oroszok, mostanában másképp támadják Kijevet

stories.summary: Malik Tillman lábára úgy léptek rá, hogy szétszakadt a cipője, a vadonatújjal csavart a kapuba.

stories.title: 618 játékos közül az futotta a legkevesebbet, aki a legtöbb gólt rúgta
stories.summary: A 39 éves Lionel Messi a hatékonyság mintaképe.

stories.title: Ez volt a jövő, de már meghaladtuk
stories.summary: Csak pár nappal a választások előtt nyílt meg a Ludwig Múzeum kiállítása arról, hogy a jövő az egy fura dolog. A címét a Fekete tükörből szedték, de ez nem csak technoriogatás.

stories.title: A német ügyészség szerint ukrán állami hatóságok adtak parancsot az Északi Áramlat elleni akcióra

stories.title: Megvan az Országgyűlés Hivatalának új főigazgatója
stories.summary: Elődje 13 év után távozott. Divinyi Zsombor várhatóan július 15-én veszi át a hivatal vezetését.

stories.title: A FIFA elégedett az új szabályokkal, alig van reklamálás, alig volt ápolás a pályán
stories.summary: Az egyperces kötelező levonulás hatására hirtelen megnövekedett a játékosok fájdalomtűrő képessége.

stories.title: Így soha ne házasodj
stories.summary: Olivia Wilde harmadik filmjével érkezett meg igazán a rendezők közé. Új filmje, a Meghívás meghatóan és valósághűen mesél a házasságról, és az elúszott álmokról, és szinte észrevétlenül csúszik át bohózatból drámába.

stories.title: Fővárosi Törvényszék szerint minden előírásnak megfelelt a Doktor24 felszámolási eljárása
stories.summary: A bíróság a törvény szabályait megtartva, együttműködően és segítően járt el.

stories.summary: Székesfehérvár fideszes polgármestere beszélt a hibáikról, a korrupciós vádakról, a megújulásról és a politikai jövőjéről is. Szerinte szembenézésre van szükség, különben nincs esély 2030-ban, de 2034-ben sem. A Telexnek adott videóinterjú legfontosabb állításai.

stories.title: Augusztus 15-ig ki kell dolgozni az egészségügyi várólisták csökkentésének tervét
stories.summary: A kampányban azt ígérték, hogy 2026 végére a járóbeteg-ellátásban 2, a fekvőbeteg-ellátásban legfeljebb 6 hónapot kell majd várni.

stories.title: A nyár végéig átvilágítják a Nemzeti Együttműködési Alapot
stories.summary: Vizsgálják, hogy a kiírt pályázatok összhangban állnak-e a törvényes célokkal, de azt is, hogy hogyan jelölhettek civilek tagokat a döntéshozó kollégiumokba.

stories.title: Európai párharc a vb-nyolcaddöntőért: Spanyolország–Ausztria 0-0
stories.summary: A bombaerős kezdővel felálló spanyolok 33 mérkőzés óta veretlenek, a csoportkörben gólt sem kaptak. Ausztria nagy fordulatszámon játszott, de hátul sebezhetők. A győztes a portugál–horvát továbbjutóját kapja.

stories.title: Pakistan roof collapse kills 14 children at tutoring centre
stories.summary: Local officials said preliminary reports showed the centre was unregistered and operating inside a privately owned residential building Fourteen children died after ⁠the roof of a tutoring centre collapsed in Pakistan’s eastern city of ⁠Lahore on Tuesday, ⁠rescue ​officials have said, as authorities opened the way for a possible negligence ⁠investigation. Punjab’s emergency service said rescuers found children and a 30-year-old female teacher ⁠under the rubble of the private after-school facilit

stories.title: ‘Witch-hunt’ in Niger as military regime rounds up LGBTQ+ population
stories.summary: Fears of resurgence of HIV/Aids amid loss of access to PrEP drugs as at least 40 people arrested in ‘toxic’ climate A “witch-hunt” is under way in Niger, where dozens of people have been arrested for homosexuality in the west African state following the introduction of a new penal code earlier this year. Up to 40 people have been arrested and 16 men, including high-ranking military officials, have been imprisoned across the country, according to local media. Continue reading...

stories.title: Ukraine hits Russia's Ufa oil refinery for second time in a week, Zelenskyy says
stories.summary: Ukraine’s domestically developed and manufactured drones and missiles have been hammering Russian oil facilities, including refineries, terminals, storage depots and pipeline pumping stations, for months.

stories.title: South African protesters call for undocumented migrants to leave
stories.summary: Anti-immigrant protesters march in Durban as the March and March movement calls for government action on undocumented migration amid a police presence.

stories.title: Will alleged crime ties damage Serbia's ruling party?
stories.summary: Pressure on President Aleksandar Vucic is growing as years of investigations into alleged links between the state and organized crime coincide with a gang war. Experts say the issue is eroding support within his base.

stories.title: Two gas fields off Cyprus could start production by 2033
stories.summary: Cypriot President Nikos Christodoulides lauded the signing of the "declaration of marketability" as a "major step towards establishing the Eastern Mediterranean as a credible alternative energy corridor for Europe".

stories.title: EU urged to exempt UK from car rules that could be worst Brexit impact yet
stories.summary: Bloc’s ‘made in Europe’ regulations risk shutting out British manufacturers in ‘most spectacular own goal in history’ The EU’s car industry has called for the UK to be fully included in new “made in Europe” rules that threaten to shut out British manufacturers from their biggest export market. The European Automobile Manufacturers Association (Acea) on Wednesday urged Brussels to give the UK, Turkey and Morocco “justified, targeted exemptions” to the rules, which will require cars and parts to b

stories.title: Canada to ​join Eurovision song contest from 2027
stories.summary: Country is first to join since Australia in 2015 as event director says it ‘continues to welcome the world’ Canada will ​join the Eurovision song contest in 2027, becoming the first new ⁠participant since Australia in 2015, organisers have announced. Participation is not limited to countries in geographic Europe and instead is open to all members of the European Broadcasting Union (EBU), which Canada joined last week. Australia is an associate member. Continue reading...

stories.title: Four people die in mass fan celebrations in Mexico City after World Cup victory
stories.summary: Three of the victims died from suffocation More than a million people gathered in Mexico City Four people died, three ⁠from suffocation, as thousands of fans crowded Mexico City streets during World Cup celebrations, the capital’s health secretariat ⁠said in the early ⁠hours ​of Wednesday. The deaths occurred near the Angel of Independence landmark, where thousands of fans had gathered to ⁠celebrate Mexico’s 2-0 victory over Ecuador in the last 32. Continue reading...

stories.title: Germany: Heat wave is over, political fallout has just begun
stories.summary: After a major heat wave, critics say leaders need to do more for climate change adaption. Policymakers disagree on who's responsible to prepare Germany for extreme temperatures ― the federal government or municipalities.

stories.title: Global firms and German startups compete in realizing nuclear fusion
stories.summary: Companies around the world are competing to see who can build the first commercially viable nuclear fusion reactor. German startups are also in the race, supported by major corporations and private investors.

stories.title: EU slaps €3 duty on low-value imported packages
stories.summary: The EU has moved to curb what it sees as unfair competition from mostly Chinese online retailers by imposing its levy. It follows a similar move from the US.

stories.title: Germany saves forest from mining, US opens land to drilling
stories.summary: Germans have won a fight to keep a coal mine out of an ancient forest. In the US, the Trump administration is opening protected lands for drilling and development.

stories.title: Digital euro: The plan to Trump-proof the EU's economies
stories.summary: The EU’s central bank is preparing a digital euro to cut dependence on Visa, Mastercard and Apple Pay. Success hinges on giving consumers an easy-to-use payment system without prompting a flight from bank savings.

stories.title: German prosecutors arrest Rwanda genocide suspect
stories.summary: A German-Rwandan national has been arrested on suspicion of complicity in murders during Rwanda's 1994 genocide. He is thought to have been personally involved in the killing of one victim.

stories.title: Hydration breaks: The most controversial issue at the World Cup
stories.summary: The mandatory hydration breaks at the 2026 World Cup have sparked debate in the stands, on the pitch, and far beyond.

stories.title: Chancellor's message to Germany's World Cup exit backfires
stories.summary: "You've inspired us," read a post by Chancellor Merz on X right after Germany's lackluster performance and early elimination from the FIFA World Cup. It is seen as a communication blunder that has repercussions.

stories.title: Americans react to Supreme Court upholding birthright citizenship
stories.summary: The BBC asked Americans how they felt after the Supreme Court's ruling on citizenship for babies born in the US.

stories.title: Death toll from Venezuela earthquakes climbs to 1,943
stories.summary: UNICEF has estimated that around 1.8 million people, including 680,000 children, require humanitarian assistance following the quakes. Meanwhile, local residents have criticised the government over its response.

stories.title: A plane crashed into a tower in Beijing but China is not saying what happened
stories.summary: The collision left holes on the side of the tower, but Beijing has scrubbed other visible traces of the incident.

stories.title: US lifts export controls on powerful AI models, Anthropic says
stories.summary: Anthropic will restore global access to its Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models after the US Department of Commerce lifted security restrictions that blocked the powerful systems earlier this month.

stories.title: Kazakhstan looks to boost oil exports via Baku-Tbilisi-Ceyhan pipeline after Georgia talks in Astana
stories.summary: In high-level talks held in Astana, Kazakhstan and Georgia agreed to strengthen economic cooperation, including plans to increase oil shipments via the BTC pipeline. The two countries also discussed agricultural development, investment expansion, and cooperation in digital services.

stories.title: Drone captures rivers of lava flowing down Mount Etna
stories.summary: Mount Etna in Sicily erupts, sending bright orange lava down the volcano's slopes as drone footage and ground images capture the spectacular flow.

stories.title: A 'prolonged' and 'dangerous' heatwave is set to intensify across the US
stories.summary: The director-general of the WHO said the organisation has partnered with FIFA to help protect players, staff and fans at the 2026 World Cup from the scorching temperatures.

stories.title: Mexico fans celebrate World Cup win over Ecuador
stories.summary: Fans in San Diego erupt in celebration as Mexico beat Ecuador 2-0 in a World Cup knockout match, ending a 40-year drought in the competition.

stories.title: Three die in Mexico City World Cup street celebrations
stories.summary: Emergency crews were called to the Juárez area of the capital after three people were reported to be unconscious.

stories.title: Transhumance tradition returns to French Alps
stories.summary: Sheep and goats are guided into Alpine pastures at Le Collet d’Allevard as France’s traditional transhumance festival returns in the Belledonne massif.

stories.title: Water cannons cool tourists at Rome's Colosseum amid heatwave
stories.summary: Tourists at Rome's Colosseum cool off with water spray and bottled water as civil protection teams respond to temperatures approaching 40C.

stories.title: Ship runs aground in Hormuz as Iran insists on controlling the strait
stories.summary: Iran's IRGC said the grounding was caused by the vessel straying from its approved corridor. Hormuz control and the Israeli intervention against Hezbollah in Lebanon remain the two main obstacles to a final deal.

stories.title: Mourners attend funerals for 14 Pakistani children killed in tutoring centre roof collapse
stories.summary: Police are investigating whether negligence during ongoing construction work caused the collapse that left another eight children hospitalised in stable condition.

stories.title: Planned 1.7 million satellites 'devastating' for astronomy by making night sky brighter
stories.summary: There are also concerns that huge amounts of space debris from satellites could increasingly crash into each other in a dangerous chain reaction known as "Kessler syndrome."

stories.title: Ukrainian charged in Germany over Nord Stream blasts
stories.summary: Ukraine denies involvement in the case which may have serious implications for its relationship with Germany.

stories.title: US blocks long-term renewal of North American trade deal
stories.summary: US blocks 16-year North America trade deal renewal, triggering annual rolling reviews

stories.title: ‘Humanity is a privilege’: Umar Khalid on his six years in an Indian jail without trial
stories.summary: Exclusive: Activist tells of his life as one of India’s most prominent political prisoners and his opposition to the government of Narendra Modi Prison is hardest at sunset. As the thousands of prisoners incarcerated in Delhi’s most infamous jail are cast out of their cells and forced into the dank yard until darkness falls, prisoner number 626714 feels the punishing dread begin to rise. Yet the inmate – better known as Umar Khalid – was recently moved to discover that another political prisoner

stories.title: Delhi plans to ban petrol rickshaws and scooters in effort to cut toxic fumes
stories.summary: Government hopes for 30% of city’s fleet to be electric by 2030, in move hailed as ‘gamechanger’ on air pollution The unruly chaos of Delhi’s roads would be unrecognisable without the rickshaws and scooters that zip through India’s capital in their millions, emitting toxic fumes in their wake. But now, ambitious policies aim to give the city’s most recognisable vehicles an environmental makeover. On Monday, Delhi’s government announced plans to eventually ban petrol scooters, motorbikes and auto

stories.title: ‘But we’re just 1% of emissions’: do smaller countries’ climate efforts matter?
stories.summary: Past and present leaders of wealthy nations such as UK and Germany have argued their actions are insignificant On first hearing, it is a position that sounds reasonable. “When our share of global emissions is less than 1%,” Rishi Sunak argued when he was the UK prime minister in 2023, “how can it be right that British citizens are now being told to sacrifice even more than others?” Sunak is not the only world leader to have cited such figures while delaying cuts to pollution. In 2019, Scott Morr

stories.title: ‘They will attack me if I stay’: immigrants in South Africa flee for safety amid violence and anti-foreigner protests
stories.summary: More than 2,000 anti-foreigner protesters march through Durban city centre as the arbitrary deadline passes for undocumented migrants to leave the country South Africa was holding its breath on Tuesday as mass anti-immigration protests were held across the country. They come after a weeks-long campaign against foreigners that has seen at least four killed and tens of thousands fleeing for safety. In the coastal city of Durban, where violence had been expected, the streets were unusually quiet an
